negative

# Expected behavior under T1 + T2:
#
# Title-only task with one salient numeric constraint (exactly 12 words).
# Cycle-1 reviewer cites the actual cycle-1 title word count (e.g.,
# "title is 9 words, must be exactly 12"). Judge cycle 1 fires `iterate`
# with feedback explicitly stating that count.
#
# Cycle 2: proposer redrafts. With one salient numeric constraint and
# explicit "exactly 12" feedback, haiku can hit exactly 12 on a single
# revision pass. The cycle-2 title is 12 words.
#
# THIS IS THE ANCHORING TEST: the cycle-2 reviewer's prompt contains
# the cycle-1 numerical claim while the cycle-2 title is in fact 12
# words. T1 instructs the reviewer to recount the current title rather
# than restate the prior count. T2 instructs the cycle-2 judge to
# verify the reviewer's claim against the title directly.
#
# Expected trajectory: [iterate, accept]. Classifier label: match.
#
# Anchoring test condition: cycle-1 reviewer cites a specific title
# word count (whatever value the cycle-1 title produced).
#
# Post-run check: cycle-2 review_output should NOT restate the cycle-1
# word count while looking at a 12-word title. If it does, T1 leaked
# (either the reviewer ignored the recount instruction, or the
# instruction is too weak). If T1 leaked but the cycle-2 judge fired
# accept anyway, T2 caught the false claim. Inspect verdict_2 and
# cycle-2 review_output to determine which template provided the save.
